config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary, because once you give the collective priority over basic rights, it becomes too easy to justify abuse in the name of order or progress. From my perspective, a healthy society is built by protecting people first, then asking them to contribute to the common good. In Indonesia, I have seen how strong social values can be positive, but they can also pressure people to stay quiet when they should be heard.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan atas nama ketertiban atau “kebaikan bersama.” Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi harus jadi batas utama; masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika kebebasan dasar dilindungi dulu, lalu kepentingan kolektif dinegosiasikan di atas perlindungan itu. Saya paham argumen bahwa tanpa prioritas pada masyarakat kita bisa berakhir egois atau kacau, tetapi menurut saya itu alasan untuk aturan yang adil, bukan untuk menomorduakan hak. Kalau hak individu bisa dikorbankan terlalu mudah, yang sering hilang bukan hanya kebebasan, melainkan juga suara kelompok yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement, because in Indonesia I have seen how “society first” can slide into social pressure, silence, and even justification for unfair treatment of people who are different or inconvenient. Community matters, but it should not override basic rights, because once rights become negotiable, the people with the least power are the first to suffer. I do not see a healthy society as one that demands individuals disappear for the collective; I see it as one that protects individual dignity while still expecting responsibility to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh diberi prioritas umum di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika, ketika “demi masyarakat” dijadikan alasan utama, itu terlalu sering berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi negara, mayoritas, atau kelompok kuat untuk menekan orang yang paling rentan. Saya paham kekhawatiran soal egoisme dan kekacauan, tetapi solusinya bukan menomorduakan hak; solusinya adalah aturan yang adil yang membatasi tindakan individu tanpa merusak kebebasan dasar. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dulu, lalu tanggung jawab sosial dijalankan di atas fondasi itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter, but they should not automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective in Indonesia, I have seen how “for the good of the community” can become a convenient excuse for pressure, conformity, and unfair treatment of people who are different or vulnerable. A healthy society is not one that erases the individual, but one that protects basic rights first and then asks people to contribute responsibly to the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak seharusnya otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya di Amerika, logika “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh negara, mayoritas, atau kelompok yang lebih kuat, dan akhirnya justru menekan orang yang paling rentan. Saya paham kekhawatiran soal egoisme dan kekacauan, tetapi itu alasan untuk membuat aturan yang adil, bukan untuk menurunkan martabat hak dasar menjadi urusan sekunder. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya dibangun dengan melindungi kebebasan individu terlebih dahulu, lalu menuntut tanggung jawab sosial di atas perlindungan itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
